title: samadera indica var. brevipetala
intro: Samadera indica var. brevipetala, also known as the "Yellow Alder" or "Padaal", is a plant species in the family Meliaceae, native to tropical forests of India and Southeast Asia. It is valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, which contribute to its health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and protecting against infections. The plant is commonly used in traditional medicine to prepare various herbal remedies, including decoctions, infusions, and ointments, which are applied topically or ingested orally to treat various ailments. Regular consumption of Samadera indica var. brevipetala preparations is believed to promote overall well-being, boost the immune system, and even support the treatment of certain chronic diseases.
